description: §18.8.2 — a `match` whose subject is an `asIs`-typed value (the opaque escape-hatch type) SHALL fire E-TYPE-025: `match` requires a typed subject (enum, union, or primitive), and `asIs` carries no discriminable structure. Regression guard: an `asIs` subject must be narrowed via a type annotation before matching.
rationale: §18.8.2: `match` requires a typed subject (enum, union, or primitive); an `asIs`-typed subject is opaque and is rejected with E-TYPE-025. Narrow the type first (`let x: SomeType = ...`) before matching.
